title: fix for Florence-2 space
body:
previously Florence2 had flash attention as a requirement due to an error in *transformers* (issue with packages Forge uses rather than in Forge itself) (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Florence-2-base/discussions/4#6673ffb9436907f83a8aaf2d); the workaround was to patch the imports to remove it (https://huggingface.co/microsoft/phi-1_5/discussions/72#65a0339f64520347aa3d703c).
At some point, an update has made this patch unnecessary. And turned it into an error.

This PR simply removes the patch.

Now the Space loads again; tested a couple of functions as working correctly too.

#2299

step_plan.summary: Remove the Florence-2-specific monkeypatch that alters Transformers dynamic import resolution to drop `flash_attn`, and load Florence-2 using the standard `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(..., trust_remote_code=True)` path again.

steps.operation: Remove the Florence-2 transformers dynamic import workaround by deleting `fixed_get_imports` (and any related helper logic) so we no longer alter `transformers.dynamic_module_utils.get_imports` behavior.
steps.reason: Upstream Transformers/Florence-2 no longer requires the historical `flash_attn`-removal workaround; keeping the monkeypatch now causes the Space to error during remote-code module import resolution.
steps.side_effects: Florence-2 will use the stock Transformers dynamic import logic; if the upstream `flash_attn` dependency issue reappears in older environments, model loading could regress. Removing this function may also require deleting now-unused imports or references elsewhere in the module.

steps.operation: Remove the `unittest.mock.patch("transformers.dynamic_module_utils.get_imports", ...)` context manager around `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(..., trust_remote_code=True)` (and any now-unused `patch`/`get_imports` imports), keeping the rest of the model initialization flow unchanged.
steps.reason: The model should load with normal Transformers behavior; the patch was a temporary workaround and is now the direct source of failures when the Space starts.
steps.side_effects: Startup/import-time behavior changes (no interception of dynamic imports). Confirm the model still loads inside any existing GPU/object capture context and that inference entry points continue to work; if tests/CI cover Space startup, they may need updating only if they asserted patched behavior.
